message:
fix(config): use PID-scoped temp files for atomic config writes

The atomic write pattern (write to temp file, then rename) used a fixed
temp file name "config.yaml.tmp" which creates a race condition when
multiple br processes write config concurrently — one process can
overwrite the other's temp file before it gets renamed.

Now uses "config.yaml.<PID>.tmp" so each process writes to its own
unique temp file, preventing corruption from concurrent config writes.
This affects both set_config_value() and apply_prepared_yaml_delete().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
